mnemonics: ER Maps: Entities become Relations, Relationships link them, Attributes fill them.
keyPoints: Strong entities become tables with their own primary key.
Weak entities become tables and include the primary key of their owner entity as a foreign key.
Many-to-Many relationships require a separate linking/associative table with the primary keys of both connected entities.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to create a separate table for a many-to-many relationship and trying to implement it with foreign keys in just one of the entity tables.
Omitting the foreign key from a weak entity table back to its owner strong entity.
Incorrectly mapping composite attributes by storing them as a single column instead of splitting them into separate columns.
